critic_raw: Missingness indicators in a credit model are a red flag for target leakage. If any missingness indicator encodes information about the underwriting outcome or the collection process, the model would appear to predict default when it is actually using post-outcome information. The burden of proof should be on the authors to demonstrate with a timestamp audit that every missingness indicator is genuinely available at application time.
defender_raw: The timestamp audit is exactly what the study provides. The methods section explicitly describes the audit confirming all missingness indicators are computed at application submission before any underwriting decision. The model excludes manual-review notes, final approval decisions, and post-decision interventions by design. The feature generation pipeline is deterministic from the submission packet and bureau query response. The future-quarter holdout evaluates the frozen model on new applications. The burden-of-proof requirement is met.

debate_rounds.critic: If the timestamp audit confirms all missingness features are submission-time, the leakage concern is resolved. The audit is the correct verification mechanism. My concern about workflow dependence remains: a different lender with different documentation requirements would need to re-validate the model because the same missingness patterns might not exist in their workflow. But the study acknowledges this by restricting the claim to the same underwriting workflow.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. Workflow dependence is a real limitation — the model's missingness features reflect this specific lender's documentation requirements. The study correctly restricts the claim to the same workflow and discloses this limitation. That is a scope constraint, not a methodological flaw. The audit addresses leakage; the scope restriction addresses generalizability.

adjudication_raw: The critic accepts the timestamp audit as the correct resolution for leakage, and the workflow dependence concern is correctly reframed as a disclosed scope limitation. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: missingness leakage concern — resolved by timestamp audit
workflow dependence — correctly disclosed as scope limitation

empirical_test.condition: Audit feature-generation timestamps to confirm all missingness indicators are created at application submission; verify future-quarter test performance using frozen submission-time feature set
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Timestamp audit reveals any missingness indicator derived from post-decision or post-outcome information
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All timestamps confirm submission-time derivation; no post-decision information; future-quarter holdout confirms frozen model performance
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Most features are clearly pre-decision but one has incomplete lineage documentation
